data_sources_used: jf graphql LMPrivacyReview.requirement_applicability (batched aliases per review)
jf graphql ComplianceRequirement node lookup (batched, shared cache)

intake.project_summary:
Specialized for smooth, professional camera movements (dolly, zoom, whip pan). Offers "Mix" feature to chain motions and tools for creating user-generated content (UGC) style ads and talking avatars. For use requested by Creative X. Inputs will be provided by Casted Talent (Image/Likeness), Employee and Contigent Worker (Image/Likeness)

See AIR1320: Decision 2, Higgsfield: Production use for Marketing will not be escalated given the mitigations (eg manual review of outputs before deployed in user facing contexts).
intake.launch_goals:
Use tool for internal productivity.
Creative X team is looking to onboarding a slew of vendors which process inputs provided by Casted Talent (Image/Likeness), Employee and Contigent Worker (Image/Likeness). This will potentially include audio, video, and image data. I'm hoping we can batch all of these reviews togethe since they all function the same. Please reach out if questions related to this larger ask

system_risk_factors.name: Using or training generative AI models

questions.question: What are you doing?
questions.answer:
* Offline evaluation of a model
* Launching or changing a user-facing feature
* Using generative AI for internal purposes, analyses, or employee tools
options: Crawling data (via Spidermate, Surfguard, or a vendor)
Using a vendor for data creation or collection
Using a vendor for data annotation
Curating training data: filtering, formatting, or structuring training data
Limited evaluation ("vibe check") of model(s) from the People's Republic of China (PRC)
Launching or changing a user-facing feature
Releasing new or updated APIs that grant access to models to external developers
Using generative AI for internal purposes, analyses, or employee tools

questions.question: Which model(s) are you evaluating?
options: Qwen (All Versions but 2.5 VL 72B) internally hosted
questions.manual_review_required_reason: Per RQ77878, all uses of new non-Meta generative AI models must be assessed against the model terms and a trade legal check must be performed before use of the model

questions.question: Please tell us about the other third-party model(s): include their versions, if you will access it via API or host it internally, and what specifically you'll be using each model for.
questions.answer: Proprietary Video Reasoning Engine (Higgsfield DOP/Animate). Also integrates and utilizes other top-tier models like Kling, Wan, and Google Veo.

questions.question: What types of data are you using as inputs to your evaluation?
options: Flywheel data (includes user prompts and responses)
Link previews of user generated content
Other type of first-party user data (1PD)
Outputs of non-Meta generative models (third-party models hosted internally or not)
Downloaded data, licensed data, or data generated/obtained by a vendor
Provided by developers using the Llama Developer API

questions.question: What modalities of data are you using as inputs to your evaluation?
Which model(s) power the user-facing feature?
options: Qwen (All Versions but 2.5 VL 72B) internally hosted

questions.question: Which model(s) power the internal purpose or tool?
options: Qwen (All Versions but 2.5 VL 72B) internally hosted

questions.question: Select all that apply regarding the dataset you intend to use.
options: Meta could identify competitors in the dataset.
The dataset contains aggregated data, but is predominantly sourced from competitors.

questions.question:
I confirm that my feature will not violate the following AI prohibited practices: 

- Take advantage of people's ages, disabilities, or socio-economic statuses in ways that could influence their behavior and cause them harm.
- Evaluate or classify people based on their social behavior or inferred personality traits in ways that could result in unfavorable treatment that is unjustified or unrelated to the original context of the data collection (also known as "social scoring").
- Intentionally design systems to gauge the likelihood of a person committing a crime.
- Intentionally design systems to infer the emotions of a person in the context of education or the workplace.

raw_requirements.requirement_text: Each user-initiated content deletion request must trigger the process to delete all applicable User Data.
Users must have the ability to request deletion of their Account.

Each user-initiated account deletion request must start once the request has been submitted, unless the following grace periods apply: (1) FB/IG/Threads U16 Users in Florida (user-initiated): up to 5 days (2) FB/IG/Threads U16 Users in Florida (parent-initiated): up to 12 days (3) FB/IG/Threads/Oculus/Meta accounts: up to 30 days. Users must be able to cancel their account deletion request during grace periods.
Backups must be retained for no longer than 90 days once the deletion process completes, and backups are only kept for restorations from inadvertent Meta-initiated deletions.
Each user-initiated account or content deletion request must revoke covered third party access to that user’s data within 30 days from the deletion request.

Each user-initiated account or content deletion request must be completed within 30 days for online assets and 45 days for offline assets once the deletion process has started, where completion means deletion (or de-identification when deletion is technically infeasible), unless a documented exemption applies.
Users must have the ability to request a download of in-scope data owned by their account.
Meta must have a complete inventory of User Data.
User Data must be stored in Meta recommended environments.
Products do not allow users to identify or search for accounts by phone number or email address.
Meta limits the user data exposed to logged-out users from first-party endpoints.
Meta limits how frequently users can make requests to first-party endpoints.
Meta automatically detects and takes action on unauthorized logged-in scraping activity.
Meta collects traffic data across first-party endpoints.
Meta searches for, investigates, and responds to unauthorized scraping actors.
Meta searches for, investigates, and responds to vectors that enable unauthorized scraping.
Use Cases of User Data Sharing to Third Parties are approved and inventoried.
Users with a Meta account can request information about how their data is being shared to Third Parties.
Meta reports unauthorized scraping incidents in line with regulatory requirements.
Meta prohibits unauthorized scraping in its Terms of Service.
Meta maintains a User Data Access Policy.
Meta investigates potential violations of the User Data Access Policy by Meta Personnel.
Cloud security logs are transferred using secure methods from cloud providers to Meta's internal systems.
Meta limits Meta Personnel access to user data based on business need.
Meta maintains an accurate record of data and processing assets that are only used for Safety, Security and Integrity purposes.
Meta maintains an accurate record of data and processing assets for Ad purposes.
Meta maintains an accurate record of data and processing assets for Operations purposes.
Meta maintains an accurate record of data and processing assets for Legal purposes.
Meta maintains a complete inventory of Privacy Policies.

In the EU, Users must give consent for the combination of their data between an intra-app service and its parent CPS before the use of that intra-app service, except if the data is combined for the purposes of Safety, Security, Integrity, Family Accounting,  Operations.

Outside of the EU/UK, all personal data shared from WhatsApp to Meta must be one of the following: (a) fully anonymized via APT standards (b) purpose limited and only processed as per WA’s instructions  (c) accessed using Accounts Center for account-level data sharing, (d) processed for safety, security, integrity or family accounting, or (e) for optional features that provide transparency before data sharing.

Personal message content, call content and traffic data can only be used for messaging/calling Core Functionality, debugging, Legal, or Network Security purposes unless a documented exemption applies.
Meta will not receive user Marketing Messages interaction data from a user until that user has seen a transparency screen that describes what data Meta will receive.

Users must meet the minimum age threshold established by the service to create and access an account unless it is a Parent-Approved Account. The minimum age threshold is 13 years old unless specified otherwise. Violating accounts will be removed from the platform.
Identifiable user data from users under the age of 13 cannot be used for advertising.
Stated age information must be collected via a Neutral Age Gate (i.e. not steering people to enter incorrect values.).
User Data deletion starts by the expiry of retention limits.
Unused Assets containing User Data must be removed.
Provided Data will be retained no longer than the life of the user’s account.

Data Observed actively or passively about an actor or Derived from the processing of existing data will be stored in accordance with Standard Retention Limits.  Examples of data observed from interactions are: records of engagement activity (user liked or commented), metadata captured at the time of the action (e.g. device data, IP address). Examples of Derived data are: a user's average online duration per day, predicted preferred ads topics, or ML scores. Copying data does not derive new data.
Offsite Data will be stored in accordance with Standard Retention Limits.
Off-Meta Technologies (OMT) Data will be retained no longer than 2 years from collection or creation for any purpose except Safety, Security, Integrity or Legal.
Offsite Ads Third-Party data will be retained for no longer than 2 years from collection or creation for any purpose except Safety, Security, Integrity or Legal.
Search data will be retained no longer than 180 days from collection or creation for any purpose except Safety, Security, Integrity or Legal.
Observed or Derived Region-level Location Data will be retained for no longer than 90 days from collection or creation for any purpose except Safety, Security, Integrity or Legal.
Observed or Derived City or more granular Location Data will be retained for no longer than 30 days from collection or creation for any purpose except Safety, Security, Integrity or Legal.
Data provided for the purpose of identity and age verification will be retained for no longer than 365 days.
When users dissociate their activity on apps & websites with the OMT control, it will not be used in an identifiable fashion to show them ads.
Non-permitted descriptors will not be used for ads personalization of Housing, Employment, Credit and Financial Services (HEC-F) ads.
Users who opt out of Audience Network do not see ads via Audience Network.
Business Tools data opted out through Meta’s ePD-3PD controls will not be used for any purpose unless an approved exemption applies.

We'll only use third-party user data (3PD) activity from iOS, iPadOs, and tvOS 14.5+ that is ATT opted-out to personalize ads we show you or measure how they perform after we've removed or combined information so we no longer know it's yours.
Third party (3P) user data received with the LDU flag will only be used for approved purposes.

At the time of ingestion, Third party (3P) user data in scope of and matched to users opted out of the OBA-3PD control will not be used for ads personalization unless the user has consented to a partner allow-list override.
Meta limits the amount of user data returned from first-party endpoints.
Third party data (3PD) from unmatched users will not be used for ads personalization unless a documented exemption applies.
Meta maintains an inventory of all use cases of data sharing across accounts on different Meta Products enabled by Accounts Center.

When a user removes an account from Accounts Center, Meta stops combining their data across their previously connected accounts within 24 hours. Previous account associations are only retained for the purposes of Safety, Security, Integrity, Family Accounting, Operations.
Data shared between accounts using Accounts Center is not used for business purposes. Business accounts can't be added to Accounts Center.

Storage and processing of training data used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences must occur within approved locations. The generation of synthetic data can occur in approved locations.
Meta determines a stable and explainable jurisdiction for users, which is consistent across connected accounts.
Safety, Security and Integrity data can only be used for Safety, Security and Integrity purposes.
Users must have the ability to turn off the Link History feature.
Users must have the ability to clear their Link History.
Third Parties receiving or accessing User Data are approved and inventoried.
Meta-owned Systems are approved and inventoried.
Contracts, Product Terms, and Third Party Policies are approved and inventoried.
Meta conducts Transfer Impact Assessment for Cross-Border Data Transfers prior to transferring User Data to Third Parties, produces dossiers and makes them available to regulators.
Meta enforces on Third Parties that do not act in compliance with Contracts, Product Terms, and Third Party Policies.
Meta monitors Third Party's processing of User Data in compliance with Contracts, Product Terms, and Third Party Policies.
Meta offboards Third Parties when the need for data sharing ceases or approved use case is no longer applicable to the sharing.

In the EU/UK for all users , and in India for users who haven’t accepted the 2021 ToS/PP, all personal data shared from WhatsApp to Meta must be one of the following: (a) fully anonymized via APT standards (b) purpose limited and only processed as per WA’s instructions (c) accessed using Accounts Center for account-level data sharing d) for optional features that provide transparency before data sharing.

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to share any user data except "3P Cookie Data, 3P Integration Data" with each 3P which is acting as a Controller (Consumer Apps), when the user is in jurisdiction "South Korea" where the third party intends to move or share the data outside of user jurisdiction.
Meta collects Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) before adding accounts to Accounts Center in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom, South Korea, Turkey, Brazil".
Meta websites must allow optional cookies and similar technologies from third parties only with user consent.
Meta must only read or write the fr cookie on a browser when the facebook.com user’s consent choices allow it.
Meta websites must have a blocking cookie banner.
Meta must obtain user-level consent for optional cookies and similar technologies through a blocking consent flow.
Meta websites must provide users the ability to change their terminal-level consent for optional cookies and similar technologies from third parties.
Meta must provide users the ability to change their user-level consent for optional cookies and similar technologies.
Meta reviews Privacy Policies annually to identify whether a content update is needed.
Meta’s inventory of Privacy Policies has accurate metadata.

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to share any user data except "3P Cookie Data, 3P Integration Data" with each 3P which is acting as a Controller (Consumer Apps), when the user is in jurisdictions "US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

A user's WhatsApp Community and Group information, WhatsApp login verification data, WhatsApp passwords/encryption keys, and Android Ad ID are not shared with any other Meta Product via Accounts Center.

User identifiable data belonging to EU end users cannot be shared or used across different CPSs or Other Services Provided Separately, without Accounts Center consent, except for purposes of safety, security, integrity, family accounting, Operations, to fulfill our legal obligations, for common or foundational AI models, or for a Specific Product.
[Residual Data] Meta will cease processing a user's residual user data within the Ads boundary within 7 days after the user subscribes to the Subscription for No Ads (SNA).

[Residual Data] Meta will cease processing a user's disallowed residual user data within the Ads boundary for specific purposes, in two ways after the user opts into 'Less-personalized ads' (LPA): 1/ Within 7 days: Meta will cease processing this data for serving inferences for users and training ranking models. 2/ Within 30 days: Meta will cease processing this data for all other applicable use-cases.
Precise location information is not collected for users under 13 globally or under 14 in Quebec, South Korea and Spain.
Users can report other accounts as being below the minimum age threshold. Violating accounts will be removed from the platform.
When minimum age enforcements are applied to an account, all other connected accounts will also receive relevant minimum age enforcements.
User-level consent for optional cookies and similar technologies must be consistent across all in-scope accounts linked via Accounts Center.
Meta must only set cookies on a user’s device from an offsite endpoint if the user is logged in to the Meta website of the endpoint.

Users under the age of 18 accessing Horizon Worlds feature from Facebook or Instagram have restricted interaction settings.  Additionally, if the "Limited Content" setting is enabled on Instagram, they are prevented from entering Age 13+ worlds on Instagram.
Meta collects Consent before adding accounts to Accounts Center in any jurisdiction excluding "European Union, United Kingdom, South Korea, Turkey, Brazil".
Meta limits Personnel's access to User Data in the Cloud based on business need.
Meta publishes a cloud security policy and a standard of minimum requirements, and tracks, reviews, approves, and documents every exception to the standard.
Meta approves, inventories, and monitors all Cloud Accounts.
Meta controls infrastructure code changes, access, and release and deployment, in Cloud environments.
Meta websites must set first-party cookies on the user’s device only after obtaining user acknowledgment through the cookie banner.
Meta cryptographically protects user passwords when stored and when in transit between users and Meta servers.
Data covered by legal hold is retained for no longer than the time period necessary to fulfil the legal obligation and is used only for legal purposes.
All non-decorative elements must have visible text or an accessibility label.
Meta includes appropriate “non-sale” provisions when sharing User Data to Third Parties.

Meta conducts regular automated scanning to detect whether any user passwords are stored in plain text within its data warehouse and takes steps to cryptographically protect, delete or otherwise render any such passwords unreadable.
Third Party data crawled for Integrity or Security Purposes is only used for Integrity or Security purposes.
Tracking Apps must present the ATT Prompt along with the ATT Meta Pre-Prompt statement.
Meta will disclose 3PD sources and purpose for processing for South Korean users upon request.
Meta SDKs and iOS apps must send IDFAs (Identifiers for Advertisers) from the user device to Meta’s servers only via the tracking domains listed in their privacy manifest.
Meta Apps opt out of Apple Intelligence Writing Tools.
Each Meta SDK and each Meta iOS app must have a privacy manifest declaring its data usage, RRAPI usage, and tracking information.
For each Meta iOS app, the data usage declaration in its privacy manifest (as part of RT16242) must match the app’s Privacy Nutrition Labels published in the Apple App Store.
WhatsApp does not use cookies on third-party websites.
Meta educates its employees about the risks of unauthorized scraping.
Meta promotes development of industry best practices to combat unauthorized scraping.

Meta honors the Global Privacy Control signals by ceasing the sharing of User Data with Third Party ad vendors for purposes of Targeted Advertising in Colorado, Connecticut, Texas, Montana, New Hampshire, Nebraska, New Jersey, Minnesota, Maryland, Oregon, Delaware.

Non-Meta generative models may only be used for purposes permitted by the model’s terms of use. All usage must be purpose-limited to those permitted use cases, and no outputs should be used beyond the scope defined by the model’s terms.
The DAA AdChoices logo must be shown on first party web surfaces where Meta displays ads  or on third party apps where Meta displays ads when ads are using data from other apps or websites.

For users under 18 (determined by a high U18 recall predicted age or verified age), products must incorporate youth protections and/or restrict access to features and experiences that are not age-appropriate.
Consent for data processing must be collected from users under the age of 18 in Florida before they can use their accounts to access Meta products.
Social features on Meta AI are not accessible to users under the age of 18 in the following jurisdictions: Puerto Rico, Tennessee (Enjoined).
Products or features that potentially connect unknown users restrict adults from initiating or engaging in unwanted or unsafe interactions with minors.

Users under 18 have access to user controls to restrict, block, mute, limit, and filter unwanted interactions, content, and communications, with special emphasis on protecting themselves from bullying and harassment.

Meta does not deny or restrict access to, nor permit policies or systems that have the effect of disproportionately denying or restricting access to, platform features or services based on users' protected class characteristics.
Meta implements Rights Manager where it has a legal obligation to do so.
Meta protects government classified, controlled, or sensitive information in accordance with applicable legal and contractual requirements.
Meta complies with contractually-mandated cybersecurity requirements in government contracts, including applicable defense and federal security standards.
Meta complies with the material terms, conditions, and legal requirements applicable to government contracts.
Meta ensures that first-party wellness features do not provide clinical diagnosis, treatment recommendations, or crisis intervention without appropriate safeguards.

Meta provides mechanisms for rights holders to report intellectual property infringement. Meta processes valid reports by removing or disabling access to the potentially infringing content and notifies the affected user; or, in case of a valid report, denies the request. Regardless of the outcome, Meta notifies the decision to the reporter.. Meta also provides a process to appeal the decisions, both to the user whose content was impacted, and to the reporter whose request was denied.
Meta provides access to crisis support resources when users engage with content or features related to mental health crises.

Meta does not engage in practices that are unfair, deceptive, or misleading (or likely to mislead), or that could materially harm consumers, including through false claims, vague representations, practices, or omissions.
Meta provides an explainable measurement of screen time spent by accounts of all users on family of apps.
Data is Anonymous only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.
Text from 1st party data is anonymous for offline data processing only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.
Personal data shared from WhatsApp to Meta is anonymized only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.
Personal data shared from WhatsApp to Meta is anonymized only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.
raw_requirements.reason: This requirement is within the enforcement SLA. SLA Timestamp: 1778184019

raw_requirements.requirement_text: Derived user data that has transitioned ownership must meet the sufficiently derived bar.
Text from 3rd party data is anonymous for offline data processing only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable
Unstructured Data is Anonymous only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.
[Escalation only] Consent experiments exclude users in high risk jurisdictions and are not related to high risk topics.

Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) experiences must provide an option to withhold consent for the data processing.  Withholding consent can disable the feature (or product, or app) or enrol the user into an alternative version of it, but only to the extent to which that data processing is needed for the feature (or product, or app) to function.
Consents experiences inform users about the choice they are making.

A consent initiated solely by Meta can be shown 1 time per year; if the user explicitly declines it cannot be shown again for 1 year.  A consent initiated following a user action which Meta considers implies intent to use a feature for which a consent is required can be shown up to 6 times in 1 year; if the user explicitly declines it cannot be shown for 180 days.  No limits apply to consents initiated directly by user action.
Meta informs and obtains deemed agreement to material updates to the Meta Terms.

Meta collects sufficient information to demonstrate what a user agreed to when they agreed either affirmatively or by deemed agreement to any version of Meta Terms or Supplemental Terms; and the Meta Privacy Policy where affirmative agreement is required;  this information is stored at least until the account is deleted.
Meta informs and obtains deemed agreement to the Supplemental Terms when the user uses the applicable product for the first time.

Meta informs and obtains affirmative agreement to Supplemental Terms: globally at the point of payment every time a payment to Meta is required or payment to a user is made (including all partner monetisation).
[Escalation only] Meta does not obtain affirmative agreement for updates being made to the Meta Terms.
Meta informs and obtains affirmative agreement to the Meta Terms when a user creates an account.
Meta informs and obtains affirmative agreement to the Meta Privacy Policy when a user creates a Meta account, unless exempted.
Meta informs and obtains deemed agreement to the Supplemental Privacy Policy when the user uses the applicable product for the first time.
Meta maintains a record of instances where User Data is shared to Third Parties, on an individual transfer basis.

Meta provides an opt-out mechanism to share User Data to Third Parties for purposes of targeted advertising on third party applications or websites in all jurisdictions except California (All), EEA (All), UK (All), Florida (U18), Puerto Rico (U18), Texas (U18), Maryland (U18), and Oregon (U16) where users are automatically opted out.
Meta collects and honors user permission to share any User Data to Advertisers and Publishers.
Meta maintains a record of user data fields that can be accessed by Third Parties.
User Data will be deleted or anonymized within 90 days upon receiving a valid deletion request from a Third Party.
Unlicensed 3PUD will be stored less than 90 days from creation.
Unlicensed 3PUD will not be used for Ads or GenAI.
Unlicensed 3PUD will not be collected from high risk geos (UK, EU, Turkey, Brazil, Australia, Korea).
Unlicensed 3PPD that is not linked or matched to a Meta User account must be anonymised or deleted within 7 days.
Unlicensed 3PUD will not be collected from u18s in New York state.
When crawling, Meta will only collect publicly available data without logging in.
Meta’s collection of User Data from Third Parties is approved and inventoried.
When crawling, Meta will respect a site’s robots.txt.
When Crawling, Meta will not collect data from high risk domains.
When crawling, Meta will use an identifiable user agent and ip address to collect any data.
Meta maintains a record of instances where User Data is received from a Third Party.

Data collected from Search Engine Results Provider (SERP) must be limited to search results URLs and titles, and only from allowed SERP providers (DataForSEO, SerpAPI, Oxylabs). The "description" field must not be collected or stored. The "title" and "URL" fields must only be used to measure search quality.
Meta's Privacy Policies inform data subjects about their privacy rights and provide accurate and clear information about how Meta collects, uses, retains, and shares personal information.
When implementing a new data processing practice, or significantly changing an existing one Meta ensures sufficient transparency about the new or changed data processing practice is provided.
Meta sends one-off notifications to users where required by regulators or court orders.

When implementing a new data processing practice or significantly changing an existing data processing practice in a way that could reasonably surprise or confuse users, Meta provides sufficient in-product transparency about the data processing practice.
Meta provides sufficient transparency about the audience of non-private user data before the data is visible to that audience.
Meta provides users with sufficient notice about current data practices annually.
Meta provides users with sufficient notice about material updates to the Meta Privacy Policy before they become effective.

Meta provides real-time notice to bystanders via a clear signal (LED, notification, etc) when collecting bystander data via a wearable device, unless the captures have faces obfuscated before being accessible to users and Meta Servers.

User Data is not matched across accounts on different Meta Products unless the user has added those accounts into the same Accounts Center, except if the data is matched for the purposes of Safety, Security, Integrity, family accounting, or Operations.
Meta collects sufficient information to demonstrate what a user consented to when they made or changed a consent choice; this information is stored at least until the account is deleted.
Users must have the ability to revoke Consent for the processing of their User Data.
Meta must identify potential processing of restricted data.
Restricted Data is not used to show users Ads.
Restricted data collected without consent can only be processed for Safety, Security, Integrity, Legal purposes.
Financial information data processing is protected via standard security mechanisms.
Phone numbers collected for security purposes must only be used for Safety, Security, and Integrity purposes.
Face landmarks and meshes used for AR effects, palm templates, and EMG sensor data are not stored on Meta servers unless users provide consent.

A user's personalization profile must not contain inferences of restricted characteristics.  A user's personalization profile may recall restricted characteristics about them or someone else as long as the restricted characteristics are (1) sourced from data the user shared with MetaAI or on FoA and (2) not linked to another user's Meta account ID.
When responding to a user-initiated request to generative AI experiences, Meta must not use 1P data beyond the conversation to make inferences about other Meta users relating to restricted topics.
Restricted Facebook Group Data is not used to show users Ads.
South Korean Resident Registration Numbers (RRN) in Government IDs must not be processed for any purpose.
EMG data collected without consent can only be processed for Core Functionality.
Restricted data collected with consent can only be processed for purposes the user provides consent for.
A user's personalization profile must not recall restricted characteristics.
Generative models that power generative AI experiences can only be trained on interaction data (aka flywheel data) collected after users were given necessary disclosures.
Generative AI experiences will only be available to users in approved regions gated by Privacy Jurisdiction.
Meta takes steps to ensure that third-party data (3PD) associated with an approved objection does not appear in generative AI experiences output.
Meta Unique Identifiers must not be used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences.

Generative models that power generative AI experiences take steps to prevent the output of content that contains non-public personal information that could be used to compromise a person's physical, digital, or financial security, or disclose a person's location.

Generative AI features must not create the likeness of a specific person unless one of the following is true: (a) Meta has obtained consent from the specific person, (b) the likeness is provided by a user prompt, (c) the person is included in Meta's list of public figures and is 18 or older, or (d) the person is included in Meta's list of public figures, is under 18, and the output is non-photorealistic.
First-party User Generated Content with faces or voice clips from Texas or Illinois (TX/IL) residents may not be used to train AI models unless user consent is obtained.
Crawled data may only be used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences if the data complies with Meta’s robots.txt policy.
Generative AI models at Meta prevent the generation of copyright infringing outputs, including rejecting requests to commit or facilitate copyright infringement (in violation of CRS Policies).

Llama Developer API Customer Content and Customer Data can only be used by Meta for core functionality, product improvement and new product development insights, legal, operations, safety, security, and integrity purposes, not to include training of integrity models. If Zero Day Retention (ZDR) is enabled, then Customer Content & Customer Data will only be used for enforcement of the Acceptable Use Policy & to comply with legal obligations.
Meta may only use Llama Developer API Customer Content or Customer Data for ads with explicit user agreement.
Meta may not use Llama Developer API Customer Content and Customer Data or data from Horizon Managed Services accounts for training generative AI models without explicit user agreement.
Datasets containing high risk CBRNE content must not be used for training generative AI models at Meta.

Do not train generative AI models on name/face pairs (images or videos combined with text containing a full name) unless (a) the name corresponds to a Public Figure, and (b) the image or video was not posted by the Public Figure’s own account.

Images and videos captured for AI experiences using wearable devices without an LED hardware tie must obfuscate faces before anyone at Meta, or its contractors, can access them, and before inference. Additionally, high-risk identifiers in media may be used for inference but must be removed before that media is reviewed by human reviewers or is made available to users.
Llama Developer API customer data and customer content must be separated, except for legal, operations, safety, security, integrity, billing, or customer support purposes.
Crawled data may only be used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences if data from high risk domains is removed.
Downloaded and licensed third-party data may only be used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences if the dataset meets our standard for use.
For users in the EU Privacy Jurisdiction, high-risk identifiers must be removed from User Generated Content before being used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences.
Special AI Character types must not have access to monetization features or interact with users under the age of 18.
Meta-created datasets containing images or videos must remove biometric identifiers (e.g. faces), remove PII, and be filtered for CSAM and CSE prior to publication.

Internal data may not be used to train externally-facing generative AI models. Internal DSS-4 or a/c priv data may not be used to train internal models, unless the data is filtered Zoom meeting artifacts.

Open graph title and description tags from link previews of news articles may only be used for inference and model training when (a) the link was posted by a reghub page in an approved market, and (b) inference happens in response to a user action.
AI characters representing licensed professionals must present users a persistent disclaimer.
Generative AI experiences released to users must meet Trust Release Requirements (launch bar).
Generative models that power generative AI experiences must not be trained on 1PD if the user’s account is restricted based on jurisdiction, age, or objection (as defined in the Requirement Bar).
Before using AI interactions (aka flywheel data) to train generative models that power generative AI experiences, high-risk identifiers, and disallowed third-party data (3PD) must be removed.
Do not use content disallowed by Rights Manager to train generative models that power generative AI experiences.

Before a user interacts with a generative AI experience, Meta ensures users acknowledge they agree to the AI Terms and provides contextual in-product transparency to explain data practices relevant to that generative AI experience.
CSAM and CSE must not be used for AI model training or evaluation.
Generative models that power generative AI experiences must not be trained on 1PD User Generated Content with an audience other than public.

Non-Meta generative models originating from the People's Republic of China (PRC) must be reviewed on a case-by-case basis and must be hosted by Meta. Outputs must be assessed and mitigated to address bias risk where required.
Personal information may only be used to personalize a generative AI experience in private contexts (e.g. 1:1 threads between that user and Meta AI).
Datasets from YouTube crawled by Meta must only contain allowed content and can only be used for inference.
raw_requirements.reason: This requirement is within the enforcement SLA. SLA Timestamp: 1776013456

raw_requirements.requirement_text: When acquiring third-party data for use in AI model development, Meta scans for Known CSAM and CSE, and Novel CSAM.
Meta servers must not have the ability to access end-to-end encrypted content, including message/call content or inferred message/call content, unless a documented exemption applies.

Chat threads must have settings that allow chat participants or administrators to disable anyone from sharing personal messages and/or E2EE messages with others outside of the chat, including with AI at Meta.
WhatsApp LIDs cannot be exposed to businesses via Cloud API. Cloud API may expose business-scoped user identifiers (BSUIDs).
When a WA user has set a username, Cloud API responses may not contain the user’s phone number unless the business has demonstrated knowledge of the phone number.
Business message content, call content and traffic data can only be used for messaging/calling Core Functionality, debugging, Legal, or Network Security purposes unless an approved exemption applies.
Ad signals from WhatsApp users who opted out of the 2021 Privacy Policy update will not be collected from Click to WhatsApp Ads.
Ad signals from WhatsApp users who opted out of the 2016 Terms of Service update will not be collected from Click to WhatsApp Ads.

In business chats, message content sent by a consumer, including data derived from messages sent by the consumer, can only be used for Core Functionality, Safety, Security, Integrity, Analytics, Product Improvement, or Operations purposes.

For business messaging products where Meta or Whatsapp are acting as a service provider, Meta or WhatsApp will only use the user data of consumers it processes on behalf of and at the instruction of the business.
WhatsApp personal messages, personal calls, community chats, status updates, and chats with certain businesses are end-to-end encrypted.
WhatsApp does not collect a user's precise location.
Products which are built with Private Processing must provide in-app transparency for the initiating user whenever Private Processing is used.
Products which are built with Private Processing must provide users with the ability to view data shared, including prompts sent, and secure session setup details.
Meta and WhatsApp must not have the ability to route a specific Private Processing user request to a specific server.
Initiating users must opt in before first use of Private Processing. Users must also be able to opt out of AI features that use Private Processing.
Private Processing must prevent Meta, WhatsApp, or anyone but the initiating user from accessing messages shared with Private Processing or responses generated.
A user must engage with Meta AI to trigger each instance of sharing their data with Private Processing.

When a user invokes a feature that shares more than one personal message at a time with AI via open server, it must be apparent to the user before sharing occurs (1) which messages are being shared and (2) any non-obvious data use such as training.
User clients must verify the software running on Private Processing servers prior to each client-server connection.
WhatsApp must publish the binaries that run Private Processing in a publicly accessible location.
Personal message content/call content can only be used for Core Functionality, Safety, Security, Integrity or Operations purposes.
Private Processing must neither store user data (e.g. prompts, messages) nor retain access to user data after a user’s request is complete.

In the AI link preview feature, only the URL and the corresponding webpage content can be used to generate the summary. The generated summary can only be used for core functionality, operations, safety, security & integrity purposes.
When a user uses the AI-link-preview feature, only the URL of the link and webpage content are shared to Meta Servers.
Any data used or produced with AI link preview cannot be associated with the user’s UserID or any other UII.
Businesses can only message a user via a scaled chat once the user has engaged with the business.
Data will only be shared with Meta if the consumer opens a Whatsapp chat thread with an advertiser within 7 days of interacting with an advertiser's ad on Meta.
A user’s choice to receive marketing messages will be respected.

Hashed contact info imported from ads business tools (e.g. Pixel, Conversions API, App Events SDK) into Marketing Messages may only be used for (a) creating Marketing Messages subscriber pools or (b) allowing the business to download the hashed contact info.

For E2EE personal chats, no more than 50 historical messages may be shared with an Al via an Open server per user invocation. For Open personal chats, no more than 30 days of historical messages may be shared with an Al via an Open server per user invocation. Up to 10 future messages may be shared per user invocation. When AI is added as a group participant, the same historical message context window limit applies and the AI has access to all future messages until the AI is removed from the group.
When users delete, hide or report a conversation or when a user blocks another user, other users will not be notified  that the user took that action.

In order for user data to leave the Private Processing (Trusted Execution Environment): (1) the user must receive transparency that data will leave Private Processing before it leaves AND the user must be offered a control to stop the data from leaving Private Processing and (2) the user should have visibility into what data is leaving.
Personal chats and calls on Messenger are end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) by default.
Users are provided controls for features which perform on-device processing of end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) messages.
Meta will not alter end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) messages in Messenger without providing control to users.
Users have the ability to verify the authenticity and integrity of end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) messages.

For any Messenger end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) message content leaving the device: (1) it must be apparent to the user which messages will be shared before they are shared, and (2) the user must have had to initiate the transfer.
Messenger provides transparency on end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) practices and standards through external communications.
Users are notified that chat participants can choose to share messages outside the end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) chat thread.

Messenger end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) message content cannot be shared with another app or device unless (1) the user explicitly chooses to enable this, (2) the app or device does not share the message content to any server, and (3) the user has the ability to revoke the app/device’s access to their E2EE messages.
A user’s precise location when collected within the messaging space is only used for Network Security purposes unless an approved exemption applies.
Wearable devices with display do not store or transmit recording or transcript text from Live Caption of audio calls.

When a user invokes a feature that continuously shares new personal messages with AI via open server, Meta must provide a zero-click persistent transparency to all participants (initiator and non-initiator) whose messages are shared.
Message content in transit can only be used for core functionality purposes, unless sufficient transparency has been provided to users describing processing conducted for additional purposes.

A user’s personal data is not processed by WhatsApp for ads on WhatsApp until the user has been provided with transparency about the Updates Tab Privacy Policy and accepted the Updates Tab Terms of Service.
Meta AI cannot be added and is removed from the group chat if at least one participant is from select restricted jurisdictions.
WhatsApp does not collect message pairs except to comply with laws or unless an approved exemption applies.
WhatsApp does not show ads to users whose association with other accounts in the Accounts Center is in paused state.
Prompts and responses in an encrypted chat with Meta AI cannot be saved or stored in chat history beyond the current session.

In order for user data to leave the Private Processing Trusted Execution Environment (TEE): (1) the data must be de-identified and (2) the data leaving can be up to 100 characters per instance and up to 5 instances per prompt.
Business Event Platform data will not be used for Ads.
WhatsApp servers do not store any information from a user's address book about non-WhatsApp users unless an exemption applies.
Traffic data is stored in accordance with ePD standard retention limits.
Traffic Data is anonymized only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.

Messages sent to chats with disappearing messages enabled must not be visible or otherwise accessible to any chat participant once the user-configured amount of time has passed after all participants have seen the message. I.e. they must disappear.  Messages may be reported for 6 hours after they disappear. After this the messages must be deleted.
Users in India must explicitly accept a disclosure disclosing that data is shared from WhatsApp to Meta and must have the ability to stop such data sharing at any time.
Customer conversation sentiment feedback is only visible in aggregated form.

Businesses and users will be provided a one-time disclosure explaining that business message data will be used in accordance with Meta-’s privacy policy. Persistent transparency is provided on the business chat thread to further distinguish business and consumer chats.
WhatsApp Cloud API Business chats show persistent transparency to clarify the difference in end-to-end encryption behavior.
Conversational Business AI Agent products that use 1P ads data from businesses other than the business represented by the AI Agent must implement guardrails to prevent reproduction of that data.
User data belonging to teen users will not be used for ads personalization for teens, except: Stated age, Coarse Location.
Age signals obtained from operating systems or app stores must be used and stored in compliance with both Meta’s privacy standards and the terms set by the third-party provider.
Limited data will be used to show ads to unknown age users on WhatsApp.
Settings giving users control over how their content is cross-posted across apps are honored.
Settings giving users control over who their data is shared with outside of Meta are honored.
Settings giving users control over how their data is collected are honored.
When choosing to share a video or audio asset after making edits, only the edited version is shared with other users.
Restrictions on how long user-generated content is visible or how many times it can be viewed are honored.

Screenshots,  media recordings, or other similar circumvention methods, are prevented for user-generated content that has been restricted for consumption for a limited period of time or a limited number of views.
Restrictions that limit visibility of interactions with user-generated content are honored.
When Meta prompts users to change the audience that can view their content or other user data, it offers transparency and presents the options in a way that does not bias the user’s decision.
When Meta informs a user about the audience of their data, the data must not be visible beyond that specified audience.
Settings giving users control over visibility of their account and the audience of their information are honored.

Data provided for the purpose of identity and age verification by users who have opted out of using this data for harm detection will be retained for no longer than 30 days after the verification process is completed.
Data provided for the purpose of identity verification by advertisers and meta verified users will be retained for no longer than 180 days from collection or creation.
Unmitigated third-party unstructured text data (e.g., full URLs, page titles) cannot be used in Ads Personalization or Ads Product Improvement.

When a user turns off the Enhanced Browsing Control, data from the IAB will no longer be collected and in-scope browser features will be turned off and not shown to the user unless an approved exemption applies.
A documented exemption is necessary for any experiments involving ads related to Housing, Employment, and Financial Products and Services (including Credit).
Users must have the ability to exercise their ePD-3PD opt out rights.
A user’s OBA-3PD control will be set to off on the first instance the logged-in user visits a Meta website with a valid Universal Opt-Out Mechanism (UOOM) signal.
Personalized ads based on third party data will not be delivered on the browser where a user has opted out to that delivery via the DAA WebChoices tool.
Third party user data received from a browser where the user has opted out of personalized advertising via the DAA WebChoices tool will not be used for ads personalization.
Users must have the ability to exercise their OBA-3PD opt out rights.
Third party (3P) user data provided for Business Tools Custom Audience exclusions can only be used for approved purposes.
Third party (3P) user data in the following jurisdictions: South Korea cannot be used for exclusion targeting if the user has exercised the OBA-3PD control.
Third party (3P) user data opted out by the ATT control can only be used for approved purposes.
Third party (3P) user data opted out by the OBA-3PD control can only be used for approved purposes.
Third party (3P) user data opted out by the DAA-OBA control can only be used for approved purposes.
Third party (3P) user data opted out by the Advertiser Tracking Enabled flag can only be used for approved purposes.
Third party (3P) user data opted out by the Application Tracking Enabled flag can only be used for approved purposes.
Business Tools third party activity data received with the Advertiser Tracking Enabled flag set to false from iOS 13 or earlier devices will not be used for ads personalization.

Meta will only use third party activity data from platforms iOS, iPadOS, and tvOS 14.5+ with Advertiser Tracking Enabled set to false to show the user ads or measure how they perform after we’ve removed or combined information so we no longer know it's that user’s data.
Third party data received with the Application Tracking Enabled flag set to false will not be used for ads personalization.

Custom List Custom Audience (CLCA) audiences and data derived from these audiences can only be used on behalf of the advertiser that sent the data, unless the advertiser shares it with another advertiser using the “on behalf of” tool.
Custom List Custom Audience (CLCA) data with the Limited Data Use (LDU) flag set will only be used for approved purposes.
Hashed Business Tools or CLCA data transmitted to Meta will be deleted after the match process is complete.
WhatsApp does not collect data about your activity on other company’s apps and websites.
Location targeting for Housing, Employment, Credit and Financial Services (HEC-F) ads must be radius-based and cover a minimum 15 mile geographic area (must be broader than city level).
Similar audience targeting (e.g., Lookalike Audiences, Special Ad Audiences) will not be used to personalize Housing, Employment, Credit and Financial Services (HEC-F) ads.
Excluding users based on detailed targeting interests is not allowed for Housing, Employment, Credit, and Financial Services (HEC-F) ads.
If the "Limit Ad Tracking" flag is set on a device running iOS 13 or earlier, Meta must not use third-party user data (3PD) to personalize ads shown on that device.
Audience Network ads must be visibly marked as provided by Meta.
At the time of ad delivery, if a user is opted out of the OBA-3PD control, Third party (3P) user data in scope of the control and matched to that user will not be used for ads personalization.
Contextual activity data from Audience Network received with the Advertiser Tracking Enabled flag set to false from iOS 14.4 or earlier will not be used for ads personalization.
The user must be able to access information about why they were shown an ad, directly and easily from within the ad's display.
raw_requirements.reason: This requirement is within the enforcement SLA. SLA Timestamp: 1774442418

raw_requirements.requirement_text: The user must be able to access information about an ad's payer and beneficiary, directly and easily from within the ad's display.
raw_requirements.reason: This requirement is within the enforcement SLA. SLA Timestamp: 1774473414

raw_requirements.requirement_text: Facebook will not make available Third Party data acquired from data providers as the sole Ad targeting criteria for segments on Facebook or Instagram.
Third party (3P) user data in scope of and opted out by the OBA-3PD control is anonymous only when it is sufficiently transformed to be no longer identifiable.
Third party (3P) user data for users in California received with the LDU flag will only be used for approved purposes.
Meta must use cookies and similar technologies from third parties for the purpose of advertising Meta products on third-party platforms only with user consent.
Meta must brief regulators on face data processing use cases in Australia, Brazil, the EU, South Korea, the UK, and the USA.

Phone Number will not be available as a targeting option for ads in WhatsApp. Exemption: Users who have linked WhatsApp account in Account Center may have their phone number used for ads targeting in WhatsApp.
Raw data from a user's linked third-party accounts (e.g., Google or Apple) should not be used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences.

When an AI continues to listen to the microphone while an app is backgrounded, Meta will leverage OS provided frameworks for displaying background app activity (such as LiveActivities on iOS and Notification framework on Android). Backgrounded voice sessions will automatically disconnect after a 45 second period of inactivity.
Voice sessions with an AI will only begin when the user takes an affirmative action. Users will be able to end a voice session through a voice command.
Third-party datasets containing personal information used to train generative models that power generative AI experiences must not be connected to individual Meta users.
Integrity predicted age signals can only be used for integrity use purposes.
Ads Personalization use cases of age data may only use stated age and the analytics predicted age model.
These product features should not be accessed by users under 13: viewing Gen AI content, using Gen-AI-powered tools, and certain features on WhatsApp and Messenger.

Users under 18 in Texas cannot make financial transactions without parental consent. Parents or guardians of users under 18 in Brazil (Not Deployed) must be able to turn off financial transactions for the user.

Potential Suicide and Self-Injury violations are proactively identified and removed to prevent users under 18 from exposure to content that promotes, glorifies, instructs, or graphically depicts suicide, self-injury, or eating disorders.
All critical flows must be defined and added to the MVA flow inventory.
All Meta-created (non-UGC) audio-only content must include a text transcript.
All UI elements must specify their semantic role (e.g. "Button") if an applicable role exists for the platform it is rendered on.
All form field errors must be shown in text and associated with the relevant field.
Experiences with time limits must allow users to modify the time limit.
Text that is semantically acting as a heading must be marked as a heading, and include an appropriate heading level on web platforms.
Text must be resizable up to at least 200% of its default size, without a loss of content understanding.
Every form field must have an associated label that clearly describes its purpose.
When users adjust their viewport or text size, content must reflow without truncating text or requiring both vertical and horizontal scrolling.
Pre-recorded (i.e. not live streamed) videos that are created by Meta (i.e. not user-generated content) must include an audio description track.
Content that auto plays (either visual or audio) and lasts more than 5 seconds, must have a mechanism for the user to pause or hide it.
All Meta-created (i.e. not user-generated) videos that contain audio must include captions.

A cognitive function test is not required for any step in an authentication process unless an alternative authentication method is available or there is a mechanism to assist the user in completing the authentication.
Content presented on hover or focus  can be manually dismissed by users without requiring them to move the pointer or focus.
No content has more than three general flashes or red flashes in one second.
Relational information between elements (e.g. whether an element is grouped together with others) is indicated.
Forms submitting legal or financial information, or deleting data, must be either confirmed by the user, or reversible post-submission.
Duplicate form fields are pre-filled or available to select.
Contrast between elements meets the minimum requirements for their type.
An interface must not rely solely on multi-finger or dragging gestures to perform any specific action.
All users must be notified of content or state changes.
All stateful UI elements must mark their current state (e.g. 'selected', 'checked').
Users can contact Meta with Accessibility support requests.
When content is presented on hover or focus, users can hover or focus on that content without it dismissing.
Single-key keyboard shortcuts can be disabled by users.
All interactive UI elements must be focusable by a keyboard and other assistive technologies (e.g. screen readers).
All functionality must be operable through a keyboard only, without the need for specific timing of keystrokes.
Any interaction that is dependent upon device motion (e.g. rage shake), must have both the ability to disable it and an alternative means of triggering it.
Interactive elements must be a minimum of 24x24 display-pixels in dimensions.
Information conveyed through color must convey the same information in another way without using colour.
Search products will support interventions for violating terms.
Content and entities that violate Meta's recommendation policies will be removed from recommendation surfaces on this product/feature.
User generated content will be scanned for previously identified violations of Meta's policies.
Product/feature will restrict high-risk interactions between adults and minors.

On user-facing surfaces where both AI-generated and non-AI-generated content are shown together (e.g., Facebook Feed, Instagram Feed), AI-generated content must be clearly labeled in the UI (i.e., "AI Info" or similar indicator). On user-facing surfaces dedicated exclusively to AI-generated content and with sufficient AI branding (e.g., MetaAI, Kadabra, etc.), no additional visible watermark or label is required.
Product/feature will not be accessible to high risk users/entities
Users will be able to dispute integrity decisions on their content or entities.
Product/feature will be eligible for proactive assessment of applicable violations of Meta's policies.
User generated content (e.g.hyperlinks) will be scanned for previously identified violations.
Product/feature will not be accessible to users ineligible for monetization
Users will receive notice when enforcement decisions impact content/entities they created, managed, or reported.
Generative AI model input and outputs that violate Meta's generative AI content policies will be removed.
Ads will be proactively reviewed for violations of Meta's policies
Ads and promoted content will be visibly marked by product/feature.
All user generated content/entities will be eligible for internal review by Meta in scaled, escalations, and investigations context.
All user generated content/entities will be eligible for enforcements and restorations by Meta.
Users will have access to controls that protect themselves from interacting with certain content or entities.
Advertising actors will be onboarded to Integrity systems to assess risk of integrity violations.
Generative AI product/feature will include rate limiters to deter scripted attacks
Generative AI models will be assessed for residual risks by simulating their response to adversarial attack
Projected increase in content/entity creation will be provided to Operations teams
User data must be integrated into Meta's data disclosure systems to enable user data preservation and disclosure (e.g. Switchboard).
Generative AI Product/feature will integrate with systems to support internal escalations and investigations.
Users/entities will be able to provide feedback on generative AI content to Meta for model improvement.
Product/feature will not be accessible by high risk advertisers.
Product will provide a mechanism for users to report content/entities to Meta for review.
Generative AI experiences will be distinguished from non-AI experiences.
Product/feature will include Ads in a publicly available library.
Content will be restricted according to Meta's Age Gating policies to prevent underage access.
Users/entities will receive penalties for violating Meta's policies on this product/feature.
Product/feature/profile will be integrated into Integrity systems to proactively review content and entities against Meta's policy violations.
Product has clear, intuitive interfaces and experiences that don’t lead a user to do something they wouldn’t have otherwise chosen to do.
Mature content and age-inappropriate commerce experiences will not be available to users under 18.
Features that enable the creation or modification of user generated content, including photo, video, audio and text, must comply with external reporting obligations. (e.g. integrate into SLeD).
User-initiated actions on product/feature will have limitations to deter scripted activity and spam.

Visible watermarks must be attached to AI-generated content (including images and video) at the point of creation only when legally required by the creator’s jurisdiction [currently: South Korea, Kazakhstan, and Vietnam], except for AI-native surfaces (e.g., MetaAI). Invisible watermarks and metadata must always be attached to all AI-generated outputs.
Meta implements Gating mechanisms to ensure Third Party access to User Data is authorized.
Meta's employees and systems must not programmatically access business user data of competitors for that respective product or surface, unless for an exempted purpose.
Data is Meta-inaccessible only when no Meta personnel can access the data, and any server-side executable artifacts that can access the data must be publicly verifiable to security researchers.

For WhatsApp optional features in India that share WA user data with Meta that are not essential for operating WA, users in India must be able to modify permission to share their data between WA and Meta via a feature-level persistent setting.

All features which have approval to share data outside of Accounts Center must be offered as optional features and provide transparency to the user before sharing that meets the legal requirements of that jurisdiction.

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to share any user data with each 3P which is acting as a Controller (Consumer Apps), when the user is in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "3P Data" for processing purpose "Ads" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom, Brazil, South Africa, Thailand".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to access User Data over permission-protected device APIs for processing purposes "Automated Product Personalization, Marketing" in any jurisdictions excluding "European Union, United Kingdom".
Meta collects Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data for processing purpose "Ads" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom".
Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "Precise Location Data" for processing purpose "Ads".

Meta collects and honors Consent to process User Data of type "Precise Location Data" for processing purposes "Core Functionality, Automated Product Personalization, Marketing, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good" in any jurisdiction excluding "European Union, United Kingdom, US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Consent to process User Data of type "Restricted Data (excluding Precise Location Data)" for processing purposes "Core Functionality, Automated Product Personalization, Marketing, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good" in any jurisdiction excluding "European Union, United Kingdom, Brazil, South Africa, Thailand, US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "Restricted Data (excluding Precise Location Data)" for processing purposes "Core Functionality, Automated Product Personalization, Marketing, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom, Brazil, South Africa, Thailand, US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Consent to share any user data except "3P Cookie Data, 3P Integration Data" with each 3P which is acting as a Controller (Consumer Apps), when the user is in any jurisdictions excluding "European Union, United Kingdom, South Korea, US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Consent to share any user data except "3P Cookie Data, 3P Integration Data" with each 3P which is acting as a Controller (Consumer Apps), when the user is in jurisdiction "South Korea" where the third party will keep the data in South Korea.

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "3P Cookie Data" for processing purposes "Ads, Automated Product Personalization, Core Functionality, Legal, Marketing, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good, Safety/Security/Integrity" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "Device Information (Excluding Cookie Data)" for processing purposes "Marketing, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom".

Meta collects and honors Consent to access User Data over permission-protected device APIs for processing purposes "Core Functionality, Legal, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good, Safety/Security/Integrity" in any jurisdictions excluding "European Union, United Kingdom, US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Consent to process User Data of type "Restricted Data (excluding Precise Location Data)" for categories 'Face Data', 'Eye Data', 'Voice Data', 'Hand Data'" and processing purposes "Legal, Safety/Security/Integrity" in jurisdictions "South Korea, US states: IL, TX".
Meta collects and honors Consent to process User Data of type "3P Data" for processing purpose "Ads" in jurisdictions "South Korea, Latin America (excluding Brazil), Canada, India, Vietnam".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "Precise Location Data" for processing purposes "Core Functionality, Automated Product Personalization, Marketing, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom, US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to access User Data over permission-protected device APIs for processing purposes "Core Functionality, Legal, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good, Safety/Security/Integrity" in jurisdictions "US States CA, CO, CT, DE, FL, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to access User Data over permission-protected device APIs for processing purpose "Ads" in any jurisdictions excluding "European Union, United Kingdom".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to process User Data of type "3P Integration Data" for processing purposes "Ads, Automated Product Personalization, Marketing, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent (fka Type 1) to access User Data over permission-protected device APIs for processing purposes "Ads, Automated Product Personalization, Marketing, Core Functionality, Legal, Operations, Product Improvement and New Product Development, Research for Social Good, Safety/Security/Integrity" in jurisdictions "European Union, United Kingdom".

Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent to process User Data of types "Restricted Data" of category "HCD for recognition" for processing purposes "Safety/Security/Integrity" in jurisdiction "United Kingdom".
Meta collects and honors Balanced Consent to process User Data of types "Restricted Data" of category "SCD" for processing purposes "Safety/Security/Integrity" in jurisdiction "European Union".

[SNA] User Data collected from accounts after they subscribe to the "Subscription for No Ads" (SNA) must not be utilized for any advertising-related purposes, including but not limited to ad ranking, targeting, and delivery.
[SNA] Users who have subscribed to "Subscription for No Ads" (SNA) will not see any advertisements on Meta platforms for accounts subscribed to SNA.
[Accounts Center] The ad choices "Subscription for No Ads" (SNA) and "Less-personalized ads" (LPA) will be applied across all accounts within a given Accounts Center.
[LPA] For users who have opted-in to "Less-personalized ads" (LPA), only allowed user data, collected after they opt-in, shall be used for ads purposes.
Product will display artist name, song title, and explicit tags, if available, from Meta’s licensed music library.
Artist name and song title line (i.e. the annotation) will link to the applicable artist page, channel or profile or audio page on the relevant service.
Use of music from the licensed music library is limited to 90 second clips when used with an accompanying visual.
Use of music from the licensed music library is limited to 30 second clips when the use is audio only.
Video cannot have more than 1 x 90 second clip from the same song.
Music will only be available in licensed territories.
Downloads of videos must be disabled when there is more than 60 secs of music.
Downloads of videos must be disabled when a download license isn’t available in the territory.
If audio is cached it cannot be cached for more than 5 days (and cached audio must be inaccessible to the user outside of the app).
Product will leverage reporting mechanisms to provide data on usage of music within video on both a production and consumption level.
Meta has a method to remove individual pieces of content with music where the rightsholder has objected to its use via contractual takedown form (COPS).
Meta will apply copyright scanning (as well as apply any resulting policies and product requirements) to any video and/or audio files that are uploaded by a user.
Music can be removed from this product, feature, or surface if Meta loses a music license (including appropriate user notifications).
User data must be shared with Third parties via Meta-approved systems, platforms or SDKs.
Risk classification must be reviewed and confirmed.
Third Parties must have granular risk tiers for access control in the gating mechanism.
All iOS app changes must complete Apple Feature Review prior to launch.
Products in scope for Data Production Privacy Impact Assessments (DPIA) will complete compliance reporting.
Products in scope for Specialized Integrity Risk Assessment (SIRA) will complete compliance reporting.
Products in scope for EU AI Act will complete compliance reporting.
Frontier AI models must undergo an evaluation process, mitigations, governance and decision making in line with the Frontier AI Framework.

users: (see intake — user population not explicitly delineated)

observed_signals: Intake contradiction: user_data_processing_types says NOT_PROCESSING_DATA but the project clearly processes image/audio/video data from talent and employees through third-party AI vendors.
Multi-vendor GenAI exposure: Not just Higgsfield — also integrates Kling, Luma, and Runway. Each vendor represents a separate data sharing relationship for likeness data.
Deepfake-adjacent: Generating 'talking avatars' from real human likeness using AI is functionally deepfake technology, mitigated by manual review before deployment.
Batch review request: Launch goals explicitly ask to batch multiple vendor onboardings into one review, which could obscure vendor-specific risk differences.

gaps.reasoning: This is explicitly a generative AI project using Higgsfield and other top-tier models (Kling, Luma, Runway) for video generation from human likeness. GenAI safety for deepfake-adjacent outputs is directly relevant.
Generated content (UGC-style ads, talking avatars) could violate content policies if not reviewed. The review notes 'manual review of outputs before deployed in user facing contexts' as a mitigation.
Global launch with third-party vendors (Higgsfield). Employee and talent data from Meta (potentially any office globally) sent to vendor infrastructure in unknown jurisdictions.

The tool processes human likeness (image/video of real people) to generate synthetic video content. Race, gender, age, and other protected characteristics are inherently embedded in likeness data and could be manipulated or misrepresented in generated outputs.
Meta's talent/employee image/likeness data is being sent to third-party AI vendors. Whether these vendors use the inputs to train or fine-tune their models is a relevant concern.

exposures.reasoning: EU AI Act has specific provisions for deepfake/synthetic media generation. Generating talking avatars and synthetic video from real human likeness may trigger transparency obligations under Article 50.

evidence_trail.found: Internal Creative X tool for marketing content creation using Higgsfield and other third-party AI vendors. Processes casted talent and employee image/likeness. NOT_PROCESSING_DATA marked despite clear data processing.
evidence_trail.implication: Intake self-assessment underestimates data processing scope.

evidence_trail.found: GenAI tree: offline evaluation + user-facing feature + internal tool. Uses third-party models (Higgsfield, Kling, Luma, Runway). Prohibited AI self-attestation confirmed.
evidence_trail.implication: Multiple third-party AI vendors each with their own data handling practices.

evidence_trail.found: Data types: image, audio, ad. Surface: quest. No models listed despite using multiple third-party models.
evidence_trail.implication: Entity map is sparse — doesn't capture the full vendor landscape.

evidence_trail.found: 7 of 14 in scope. External sharing and external use are key (vendor data sharing). Consent relevant for talent/employee likeness. Internal security for data transmission to vendors.
evidence_trail.implication: Third-party vendor relationship is the primary risk driver, not end-user privacy.

evidence_trail.found: GenAI safety is the top concern (deepfake-adjacent outputs). Protected characteristics inherent in likeness data. AI training data concern with third-party vendors.
evidence_trail.implication: Generative AI risks dominate this review.

evidence_trail.found: EU AI Act deepfake/synthetic media provisions likely apply. Other projected risks not relevant for internal tool.
evidence_trail.implication: Regulatory risk focused on synthetic media obligations.
